content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 3, 1991

Lloyd W. Lowrey, Jr.
Noland, Hamerly, Etienne & Hoss
Post Office Box 1818
Salinas, CA 93902

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-91-224

Dear Mr. Lowrey:

You have requested advice concerning the conflict of interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act ("Act").1/ Your request is
one for general assistance. Accordingly, we are treating it as a
request for informal assistance.2/

QUESTIONS

1. Are the directors of the Water Awareness Committee of
Monterey County (the "Committee") required to file conflict of
interest statements?

2. Are the directors subject to the Political Reform Act
directly in relation to their employment in local agencies located
within Monterey County?

CONCLUSION

The directors of the Committee are "public officials" as
defined in the Act requiring designation in a conflict of interest
code. As public officials, the directors of the Committee should
be filing statements of economic interests regardless of other
filing responsibilities pursuant to their employment in other
local government agencies for which they may again be designated.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Our File No. I-91-224
Page 2

FACTS

The Water Awareness Committee of Monterey County is a non-
profit corporation and was created to bring together and
coordinate representatives of the diverse interests in the
community and water industry from the different geographic areas
and from the public and private sector, for the purpose of
citizen's education on water and its influence on the community.

Members of the Committee serve without compensation but may
be reimbursed for actual necessary expenses incurred in the
performance of their duties.

The Committee may appoint an Executive Committee or advisory
committees as deemed necessary to study and report on specific
issues which relate to their purposes.

ANALYSIS

Section 87300 requires every local government agency to adopt
and promulgate a conflict of interest code. A conflict of
interest code is a document which must specifically enumerate
positions within the agency which involve the making of
participation in the making of decisions which may foreseeably
have a material financial effect on any financial interest. For
each such enumerated position, the code must describe the specific
types of investments, business positions, interests in real
property, and sources of income which are reportable. (Section
87302.) An investment, business position, interest in real
property, or source of income shall be made reportable by the
Conflict of Interest Code if the business entity in which the
investment or business position is held, the interest in real
property, or the income or source of income may foreseeably be
affected materially by any decision made or participated in by the
official or employee or designated employee by virtue of his or her position.3/ (Id.)

A public official "makes a governmental decision" when he or
she, acting within the authority of his or her office:

(1) Votes on a matter;

_______________
3/ Enclosed is an instructional memorandum for use by local
government agencies when promulgating a conflict of interest code.
Please contact the code reviewing body for your agency to
determine if any additional requirements are to be met by your
agency prior to the adoption of your conflict of interest code.
The code reviewing body for a single city agency is the city
council. Single-county agencies report to their board of
supervisors. All multi-county jurisdictional agencies fall under
the authority of the Fair Political Practices Commission (the
"Commission"). (Government Code Section 82011.)

Our File No. I-91-224
Page 3


(2) Appoints a person;

(3) Obligates or commits his or her agency to any
course of action;

(4) Enters into any contractual agreement on
behalf of his or her agency;

(5) Determines not to act, within the meaning of
sub-paragraphs (1), (2), (3), and (4), unless
such determination is made because of his or
her financial interest.

Regulation 18700(b)

A public official "participates in the making of a
governmental decision" when, acting within the authority of
his or her position, he or she:

(1) Negotiates, without significant substantive
review, with a governmental entity or private
person regarding the decision; or

(2) Advises or makes recommendations to the
decision-maker, either directly or without
significant intervening substantive review,
by:

(A) Conducting research or making any
investigation which requires the exercise of
judgment on the part of the official and the
purpose of which is to influence the decision;
or

(B) Preparing or presenting any report,
analysis or opinion, orally or in writing,
which requires the exercise of judgment on
the part of the official and the purpose of which
is to influence the decision.

Regulation 18700(c)

Designated employees who are required to file statements
of economic interests under any other agency's conflict of
interests code, or under Article 2 for a different
jurisdiction, may expand their statement of economic
interests to cover reportable interests in both
jurisdictions, and file copies of this expanded statement
with both entities in lieu of filing separate and distinct
statements, provided that each copy of such expanded
statement filed in place of an original is signed and
verified by the designated employee as if it were an
original. (See Section 81004.)

Our File No. I-91-224
Page 4


If you should have further questions or concerns
regarding this letter, of should you need assistance in the
amendment process incorporating the above changes, please do
not hesitate to contact the Legal Division at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

[signature]

By: Cheryl A. Hoff
Staff Services Analyst
Legal Division

SH:CAH:ch

Enclosures

sections.question:
1. Are the directors of the Water Awareness Committee of Monterey County (the "Committee") required to file conflict of interest statements?

2. Are the directors subject to the Political Reform Act directly in relation to their employment in local agencies located within Monterey County?
sections.conclusion:
The directors of the Committee are "public officials" as defined in the Act requiring designation in a conflict of interest code. As public officials, the directors of the Committee should be filing statements of economic interests regardless of other filing responsibilities pursuant to their employment in other local government agencies for which they may again be designated.

1, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18329(c)(3).)
sections.facts:
The Water Awareness Committee of Monterey County is a non-profit corporation and was created to bring together and coordinate representatives of the diverse interests in the community and water industry from the different geographic areas and from the public and private sector, for the purpose of citizen's education on water and its influence on the community.

Members of the Committee serve without compensation but may be reimbursed for actual necessary expenses incurred in the performance of their duties.

The Committee may appoint an Executive Committee or advisory committees as deemed necessary to study and report on specific issues which relate to their purposes.
sections.analysis:
Section 87300 requires every local government agency to adopt and promulgate a conflict of interest code. A conflict of interest code is a document which must specifically enumerate positions within the agency which involve the making or participation in the making of decisions which may foreseeably have a material financial effect on any financial interest. For each such enumerated position, the code must describe the specific types of investments, business positions, interests in real property, and sources of income which are reportable. (Section 87302.) An investment, business position, interest in real property, or source of income shall be made reportable by the Conflict of Interest Code if the business entity in which the investment or business position is held, the interest in real property, or the income or source of income may foreseeably be affected materially by any decision made or participated in by the designated employee by virtue of his or her position.3/ (Id.)

A public official "makes a governmental decision" when he or she, acting within the authority of his or her office:

(1) Votes on a matter;

3/ Enclosed is an instructional memorandum for use by local government agencies when promulgating a conflict of interest code. Please contact the code reviewing body for your agency to determine if any additional requirements are to be met by your agency prior to the adoption of your conflict of interest code. The code reviewing body for a single city agency is the city council. Single-county agencies report to their board of supervisors. All multi-county jurisdictional agencies fall under the authority of the Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission"). (Government Code Section 82011.)

(2) Appoints a person;

(3) Obligates or commits his or her agency to any course of action;

(4) Enters into any contractual agreement on behalf of his or her agency;

(5) Determines not to act, within the meaning of sub-paragraphs (1), (2), (3) and (4), unless such determination is made because of his or her financial interest.

Regulation 18700(b)

A public official "participates in the making of a governmental decision" when, acting within the authority of his or her position, he or she:

(1) Negotiates, without significant substantive review, with a governmental entity or private person regarding the decision; or

(2) Advises or makes recommendations to the decision-maker, either directly or without significant intervening substantive review, by:

(A) Conducting research or making any investigation which requires the exercise of judgment on the part of the official and the purpose of which is to influence the decision; or

(B) Preparing or presenting any report, analysis or opinion, orally or in writing, which requires the exercise of judgment on the part of the official and the purpose of which is to influence the decision.

Regulation 18700(c)

Designated employees who are required to file statements of economic interests under any other agency's conflict of interest code, or under Article 2 for a different jurisdiction, may expand their statement of economic interests to cover reportable interests in both jurisdictions, and file copies of this expanded statement with both entities in lieu of filing separate and distinct statements, provided that each copy of such expanded statement filed in place of an original is signed and verified by the designated employee as if it were an original. (See Section 81004.)

If you should have further questions or concerns regarding this letter, or should you need assistance in the amendment process incorporating the above changes, please do not hesitate to contact the Legal Division at (916) 322-5901.

QUESTION: 1. Are the directors of the Water Awareness Committee of Monterey County (the "Committee") required to file conflict of interest statements?

2. Are the directors subject to the Political Reform Act directly in relation to their employment in local agencies located within Monterey County?

CONCLUSION: The directors of the Committee are "public officials" as defined in the Act requiring designation in a conflict of interest code. As public officials, the directors of the Committee should be filing statements of economic interests regardless of other filing responsibilities pursuant to their employment in other local government agencies for which they may again be designated.

1, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18329(c)(3).)
